title: When your team is desynced, trade or minimize instead of forcing
body: If your team is late, dead, basing, or otherwise desynced, forcing the same side usually turns a bad situation into a throw. In those moments, the clean choices are to trade something on the opposite side or minimize what the enemy gets by protecting waves, towers, or nearby camps.
actionables: Before matching an objective, ask whether your team's tempo lines are actually synchronized enough to contest.
If they are not, choose the cleanest crossmap trade available or reduce what the enemy can take.
Review every lost objective by labeling it as a failed match, a missed trade, or a missed minimize.

source.title: Solving The #1 SoloQ Problem (How To Carry Bad Teams) — Jungle Guide
source.notes: Built from Leo's use of trade and minimize in games where teammates are dead or too desynced to contest directly.
